message:
feat(raptorq): add per-lane floor threshold for dual-addmul auto policy

Introduce addmul_min_lane parameter to prevent fused addmul performance
regression when one operand lane becomes very small relative to the
other. Track-E benchmarking discovered that asymmetric lane sizes (e.g.,
12288 + 1536 bytes) cause fusion overhead to outweigh benefits.

GF256 changes:
- Add addmul_min_lane field to DualKernelPolicySnapshot and
  Gf256ProfilePackMetadata structs
- Set floor at 2KiB for SIMD profile packs (SSE2, AVX2); scalar pack
  keeps 0 since it has no kernel dispatch overhead
- Gate fused path on len_a.min(len_b) >= policy.addmul_min_lane in
  dual_addmul_decision_with_policy()
- Add ASUPERSYNC_GF256_DUAL_ADDMUL_MIN_LANE environment variable
  override for runtime tuning
- Update dual-policy schema from v2 to v3

Benchmark changes:
- Update policy schema from v1 to v2 in telemetry JSON templates
- Add addmul_min_lane field to track-e-policy and track-e-policy-probe
  telemetry schemas
- Add benchmark scenario RQ-E-GF256-DUAL-007: (12288, 1536) with
  mul_const=211 to exercise asymmetric lane rejection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
